articles.headline: Delta VIP Travel Perks Suspended For Members Of Congress Amid Budget Dispute
articles.blurb: Extremely long wait times to get through security at US airports has been commonplace in recent weeks as a partial government shutdown has forced airport security workers to work without pay until their portion of the budget can be passed by politicians. As a result, Delta Air Lines said on...
articles.takeaway: Delta's suspension of congressional perks signals airlines are leveraging their operational challenges during the shutdown to pressure lawmakers on budget negotiations.

articles.headline: Five aviation rivalries that define competition in the modern era

articles.takeaway: Modern aviation competition increasingly blurs manufacturer-operator-supplier lines, with strategic rivalries driving innovation across aircraft development, network strategy, and technology adoption rather than simple market share battles.

articles.headline: Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk
articles.blurb: Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).
articles.takeaway: Effective risk controls require organizations to formally document and justify acceptance of remaining risks, transforming theoretical safety analysis into operational responsibility.

articles.headline: Starcloud achieves unicorn status with $170 million raise for orbital data centers
articles.blurb: Starcloud has raised $170 million to accelerate development of its next-generation spacecraft, reaching a $1.1 billion valuation as it awaits permission to deploy an 88,000-strong orbital data center network.
articles.takeaway: Orbital data centers represent a new revenue stream beyond traditional space services, attracting venture capital at scale as latency-sensitive computing moves to LEO infrastructure.

articles.headline: Best Transpacific Airlines (Premium Comfort Revealed)

articles.takeaway: Airlines are competing on premium cabin amenities and service quality to justify significantly higher ticket prices on ultra-long-haul transpacific routes where flight duration exceeds passenger endurance thresholds.

articles.headline: The Evolution of Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.
articles.takeaway: Modern SMS shifts aviation safety from investigating accidents to preventing them through systematic risk identification and mitigation before incidents occur.

articles.headline: Science finds a place on Artemis 2
articles.blurb: While the Artemis 2 mission is primarily a test flight, the four astronauts on board will conduct some science during the nearly 10-day mission.

articles.headline: Why Do Airlines Still Invest In Premium Economy Instead Of Business Class?
articles.blurb: It seems to be one of the use of space aboard aircraft for maximizing revenue.
articles.takeaway: Premium economy generates higher margins per square foot than business class while capturing price-sensitive premium passengers who won't pay full business fares.

articles.headline: easyJet Flight Attendant Injured As Turbulence Strikes Shortly After Takeoff
articles.blurb: An easyJet cabin crew member required emergency medical assistance.
articles.takeaway: Severe early-flight turbulence injuries highlight the operational risk window when cabin crew mobility is highest and safety equipment may not yet be fully secured.

articles.headline: 7 Reasons Why The F-22 Still Outperforms Newer Jets
articles.blurb: While the F-35 now outperforms F-22 as a balanced advanced fighter, the F-22 still wins in its air dominance role and some flying characteristics.
articles.takeaway: The F-22's sustained air dominance capabilities demonstrate that specialized platform design remains superior to multi-role versatility in specific operational niches.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization across airlines, airports, and flights can simultaneously address capacity constraints and environmental impact as traffic demand continues to surge.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: Why Do Cargo Airlines Still Prefer The Boeing 747 Over Newer Jets?
articles.blurb: The new generation of jets has pushed out the icons of old, but one is still hanging around and is actually preferred over many of these new options.
articles.takeaway: The 747's unmatched cargo volume and deck configuration provide superior economics for freight operators despite higher fuel costs, making payload capacity a greater profit driver than fuel efficiency in cargo aviation.

articles.headline: ‘Shots on goal and win the game’: NASA’s effort to accelerate lunar landings
articles.blurb: While NASA outlined plans to increase the cadence of robotic lunar lander missions at a recent event, it said little about accelerating work on crewed lunar landers.
